title: The Mystery of the Missing Mangoes
synopsis: When mangoes start disappearing from the village market, young detective Nia follows clues through the savanna to solve the mystery and discovers an unexpected friend who just needed help.

pages.text:
Nia loved visiting the village market with her grandmother. The colorful fruits and vegetables made her smile. Her favorite stand belonged to Mr. Kofi, who sold the sweetest mangoes in all of Kenya.

'Good morning, Nia!' called Mr. Kofi. But today he looked worried. 'Someone is taking my mangoes at night. Every morning, more are gone!'

Nia's eyes grew wide. 'That's strange, Mr. Kofi. When did this start?'

'Three days ago,' he said, shaking his head. 'I don't know what to do.'

Nia thought carefully. She loved solving puzzles and mysteries. 'Don't worry, Mr. Kofi. I'll help you find out who's taking your mangoes!'

illustration.description: A bustling village market scene painted in warm gouache colors with thick brushstrokes. Nia, a girl with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes, stands next to an elderly man at a fruit stand. Golden grass and orange sunset colors fill the background. The mango stand shows some empty spots where fruit should be.
illustration.alt: Nia talking to Mr. Kofi at his mango stand in the village market

pages.text:
That evening, Nia asked her grandmother if she could sleep outside in their garden. 'I want to watch for the mango thief,' she explained.

Grandmother smiled. 'You're a brave detective, Nia. But be careful.'

Nia set up her blanket near the fence that separated their garden from the path to the market. She brought her flashlight and notebook to write down clues.

As the sun set behind the acacia trees, Nia listened carefully. She heard the usual night sounds - crickets chirping, owls hooting, and leaves rustling in the wind.

Then she heard something different. Footsteps! But they sounded strange - not like human footsteps at all.

'Thump, thump, thump,' went the mysterious sounds, getting closer to the market.

illustration.description: A nighttime savanna scene in gouache painting style with thick brushstrokes. Nia, with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes, lies on a blanket in a garden holding a flashlight. The scene shows textured painterly golden grass, acacia trees silhouetted against an orange sunset sky, and expressive night atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Nia camping in the garden at night, listening for the mango thief

pages.text:
Early the next morning, Nia went to investigate. Near Mr. Kofi's mango stand, she found her first real clue - large, round footprints in the dirt!

'These aren't human footprints,' Nia said to herself, kneeling down to look closer. 'They're much too big and round.'

She followed the trail of footprints. They led away from the market, through the golden grass, toward the big baobab tree on the hill.

Nia also noticed something else - small pieces of mango skin scattered along the path. 'The thief is eating the mangoes while walking!' she realized.

The footprints were getting deeper in the soft earth near a watering hole. Nia knew she was getting close to solving the mystery.

'I wonder what kind of animal makes footprints like these,' she thought, feeling excited and a little nervous.

illustration.description: A morning savanna scene painted with gouache and thick brushstrokes. Nia, with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes, kneels examining large round footprints in the dirt. The scene shows textured painterly golden grass, a large baobab tree in the distance, and warm orange sunset colors in the sky.
illustration.alt: Nia following large round footprints through the savanna grass

pages.text:
Behind the baobab tree, Nia heard a soft rumbling sound. She peeked around the thick trunk and gasped!

There sat a young elephant, much smaller than the grown-up elephants she'd seen before. The little elephant was holding a mango in its trunk, but it looked very sad.

'Hello there,' Nia said gently, stepping out from behind the tree.

The elephant looked up with big, worried eyes. It made a soft trumpeting sound.

Nia noticed something important - the elephant looked very thin, and there were no other elephants around.

'Are you all alone?' she asked kindly. 'Are you hungry?'

The little elephant nodded its big head and made another sad sound. Nia's heart filled with understanding.

'Oh! You're not really a thief at all. You're just a hungry baby who needs help!'

illustration.description: A touching scene painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes showing Nia, with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes, standing near a young, thin elephant behind a massive baobab tree. The scene uses warm savanna colors, textured painterly golden grass, and expressive animal features to show the elephant's sadness and Nia's compassion.
illustration.alt: Nia discovering the lonely young elephant behind the baobab tree

pages.text:
Nia ran back to the village to get help. She found the wildlife ranger, Ms. Amara, and explained everything.

'A baby elephant alone?' Ms. Amara said with concern. 'We must help immediately!'

Together, they returned to the baobab tree. Ms. Amara brought special elephant food and water.

'This little one must have gotten separated from its family,' she explained to Nia. 'It was hungry and found the mangoes. Elephants have a very good sense of smell.'

They fed the baby elephant and gave it plenty of water. Soon it looked much happier and stronger.

'We'll take good care of you until we find your family,' Ms. Amara promised.

Mr. Kofi came to see the elephant too. When he understood the situation, he smiled. 'Any hungry baby is welcome to my mangoes,' he said kindly.

Nia had solved her first mystery and made a wonderful new friend!

illustration.description: A heartwarming conclusion scene painted in gouache with bold confident strokes. Nia, with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes, stands with Ms. Amara and Mr. Kofi as they feed the now-happy young elephant. The scene shows warm savanna colors, textured painterly golden grass, and an orange sunset sky with expressive animals and caring human characters.
illustration.alt: Nia, Ms. Amara, and Mr. Kofi taking care of the young elephant

metadata.illustrationStyle: gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes

questions.question: What was disappearing from Mr. Kofi's stand?
questions.explanation: Mr. Kofi told Nia that someone was taking his mangoes at night, and every morning more were gone.

questions.question: What kind of footprints did Nia find?
questions.explanation: Nia found large, round footprints in the dirt near the mango stand, which were much too big to be human footprints.

questions.question: Where did Nia find the mystery animal?
questions.explanation: Nia followed the footprints to the big baobab tree on the hill, where she found the young elephant hiding behind it.

questions.question: Why was the elephant taking the mangoes?
questions.explanation: The baby elephant was separated from its family and was hungry, so it took the mangoes to survive.

questions.question: Who helped Nia take care of the elephant?
questions.explanation: Nia got Ms. Amara, the wildlife ranger, who brought special elephant food and water to help care for the baby elephant.
